title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: But today we are going to tell the story and have a conversation about how we met.
She's not going to have a day with me.
Like, that's the way that this is going to work.
Basically, she was saying, I'm going to break up with you if you're not taking the show.
My family and friends thought I was a little bit crazy and not going to lie.

title: will / shall
examples: And you're like, oh, I'll bring you one.
We'll save New York and living together for next episode because I think we have some interesting stories about that as well.
Leave a comment with any questions you have and we will try to answer it in future episodes.
We will be back with more fun stories.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: I just think it's romantic to say, oh, can I take you out on a date?
Can I take you out for dinner?
I think it's super cute when you can tell the guy is trying really hard, and then everything goes horribly.
You couldn't see the view at all.
And then when you asked me, I can't do too much in the public.

title: must / can't (deduction)
examples: That must be Callie's boyfriend.
I think there was something must have been pulling us.

title: have to / must
examples: And then we had to stay at his house.

title: would (habit/conditional)
examples: So my husband and I decided that we would show you what a normal everyday conversation looks like.
And even one-on-one, I would message each other.
And actually one of my favorite memories of you when we first started hanging out is I felt like you would always make excuses to come see me.
It would be the silliest thing.
Like, I'd be like, oh, my pencil broke.

title: Passive (is done / was done)
examples: Was it in that voice, too?

title: Passive (special: get + p.p.)
examples: Yeah, Hong Kong got lost.

title: have something done
examples: But today we are going to tell the story and have a conversation about how we met.
